frame_analyses.description: Endoloop trainer visible. Blue loop material and black pusher instrument visible entering field from lower right. Three red foam appendages visible on the right side of the pegboard, clearly marked as targets.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop and pusher already loaded; timing begins with instrument on monitor.

frame_analyses.description: Blue loop and black pusher advancing toward the middle (target) appendage. Good instrument control and smooth trajectory toward target.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Straight approach trajectory to middle appendage.

frame_analyses.description: Loop is now positioned around the base/marked region of the middle appendage. Loop appears to be opening and encircling the target appendage at the appropriate level.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop placement appears centered on the marked region of middle appendage.

frame_analyses.description: Blue loop clearly encircling the middle red appendage. The loop appears properly positioned at the marked site near the base of the appendage.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop diameter appropriate for appendage diameter; position appears aligned with mark.

frame_analyses.description: Loop continues to encircle the middle appendage. Black pusher visible in control position, ready to advance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady positioning; loop secure around target tissue.

frame_analyses.description: Pusher beginning to advance distally. Loop cinching motion has begun; visible compression of the appendage tissue at the cinch point.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Advancement appears smooth and controlled; knot cinching progressing.

frame_analyses.description: Pusher advancing further; loop knot is being drawn tight around the middle appendage. Clear indentation/compression visible at the cinch site, indicating secure engagement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Progressive cinching observed; knot appears to be securing at the marked level.

frame_analyses.description: Pusher has advanced significantly; loop is now tightly cinched around the middle appendage base. Visible constriction of foam tissue confirms knot is engaged and tight.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure and at appropriate mark level; appendage properly ligated.

frame_analyses.description: Loop knot fully cinched. Pusher ready to be broken/separated at the scored mark. The knot remains secure on the appendage.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot positioning appears correct at the marked site.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic scissors or cutting instrument visible. Loop tail extends from the knot. Operator preparing to cut the trailing loop material inside the trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper positioning for tail cut; scissors approach from appropriate angle.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors cutting through the blue loop tail. Cut is being made cleanly inside the trainer box, below the knot site.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut appears to be clean and complete; tail material being severed.

frame_analyses.description: Tail cut completed. Loop material has been severed. The middle appendage remains tightly ligated with the knot secure at the marked site. Final state shows ligated appendage with no visible slack or slippage.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion confirmed. Appendage remains properly ligated; knot secure at mark.

penalties.description: None observed. Knot appears to be positioned at or within 1-2mm of the marked site on the middle appendage.
Loop remains secure throughout and after cinching. No slippage or loosening observed.
No visible tearing, transection, or excessive compression of the appendage beyond normal cinching. Appendage remains intact.

loop_placement.notes: Loop centered on target appendage at marked level near base.

knot_security.final_state_evidence: Visible indentation/compression of foam at cinch point remains constant in final frames.

confidence_rationale: Excellent visibility throughout the task. Loop placement, cinching, and final position are clearly observable. Mark alignment is difficult to assess with sub-millimeter precision due to slight camera angle, but visual evidence strongly suggests placement within 1-2mm of the target mark. Knot security is confirmed by sustained tissue compression in final state. No penalties are justified by the available evidence.
cannot_determine: Exact pusher break point—video does not clearly show where the plastic pusher was scored or broken relative to the defined mark. The pusher advancement appears complete, but the break location is not explicitly visible.
technique_summary: Exceptional expert-level performance. Task completed in 22 seconds (well below the 53-second proficiency benchmark and 20-40 second expert range). Loop properly placed on target appendage at marked site. Knot secured tightly with no slippage or tissue damage. Tail cut cleanly inside trainer. Clean, efficient instrument control throughout all phases.
strengths: Rapid loop introduction and positioning (2 seconds)—demonstrates pre-planning and smooth instrument handling
Controlled, progressive knot cinching with clear visual confirmation of tissue engagement (frames 6-9)
Secure final knot state with no signs of slippage or repositioning
Clean tail cut performed inside trainer with proper scissors technique
Excellent spatial awareness and minimal instrument fumbling
improvement_suggestions: None. Performance meets or exceeds expert benchmarks across all phases.
